justification: TechCrunch article explicitly names Yann LeCun as executive chairman of AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence) and describes the company's founding.
Wikipedia entry for Yann LeCun explicitly states he joined AT&T Bell Laboratories in Holmdel, NJ in 1988 as a researcher, where he developed convolutional neural networks.
Official Collège de France page for Yann LeCun's Annual Chair in Computer Sciences and Digital Technologies (2015-2016), directly documenting the chair appointment.
TechCrunch article explicitly names Alex LeBrun as CEO of AMI Labs alongside Yann LeCun as executive chairman.
Columbia Law School faculty page for Timothy Wu lists him as the Julius Silver Professor of Law, Science and Technology.
Stanford HAI announcement explicitly states Yejin Choi joined from her previous post as senior research manager at the Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence.
Wikipedia article on Yejin Choi documents her appointment as Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Stony Brook University after her doctorate.
TechCrunch profile explicitly identifies Heidy Khlaaf as Engineering Director of the AI Assurance team at Trail of Bits.
Karen Hao's personal website about page documents her previous role as a reporter at The Wall Street Journal covering American and Chinese tech companies.
Wikipedia article on Karen Hao explicitly states she previously worked at Quartz as a tech reporter and data scientist.
Official House Science Committee page for a 2023 AI hearing explicitly identifies Frank Lucas as Chairman making an opening statement, documenting his chairmanship role.
Official press release from Rep. Ted Lieu's office explicitly names him as Co-Chair of the House Democratic Commission on AI and the Innovation Economy.
Official press release from Rep. Lieu's office documenting the launch of the Bipartisan Task Force on Artificial Intelligence in the 118th Congress with Lieu as Co-Chair.
Official Senate page for Ed Markey lists his committee assignments including the Commerce, Science and Transportation Committee.
University of Tennessee news article explicitly identifies Lynne Parker as Associate Vice Chancellor and director of the AI Tennessee Initiative announcing her retirement/emerita status.
